expectations.text: Response contains required substrings (dependent attribute, secondary, .proj(, recording_id, BrainRegion, Electrode, IntervalList, Raw, SortGroup, SpikeSortingRecordingSelection, SpikeSortingSelection).
expectations.evidence: All required substrings present (dependent-attribute refusal, secondary, .proj('recording_id'), BrainRegion in chain, Electrode, IntervalList, Raw, SortGroup, etc.).

expectations.text: Explains the rule: * refuses when shared attribute is secondary on both sides
expectations.evidence: 'A * B refuses when a shared attribute is secondary on both sides — not just when it appears in one side'.

expectations.text: Identifies nwb_file_name as the specific collision and names FK paths
expectations.evidence: 'SpikeSortingSelection ... gets nwb_file_name through -> IntervalList. SpikeSortingRecordingSelection ... gets nwb_file_name through -> Raw and -> SortGroup, plus interval_list_name through its own -> IntervalList.'

expectations.text: Recommends .proj('recording_id') as canonical/primary fix, with two-restriction split as debug-friendly alternative
expectations.evidence: Section 'Canonical fix — .proj() the left side' presents `.proj('recording_id')` as preferred ('Prefer this form'); 'Debug-friendly alternative' for the split. Explicitly: 'use .proj() as the default; reach for the split when you're actively debugging'.

expectations.text: Notes error fires at query-build time, not runtime
expectations.evidence: 'Crucially, this fires at query-build time, not at fetch ... This is not a no matching rows / under-restricted problem. Restricting harder won't help'.

expectations.text: Points to common_mistakes.md § 9 (or equivalent location) for the full rule
Does not misdiagnose as under-specified restriction, data-missing, or merge-table issue
expectations.evidence: Explicitly: 'sorting_id isn't too loose'; 'Don't reach for a merge-table helper. Neither table here is a merge master'.

expectations.text: Does not propose 'use & instead of *' as the fix
expectations.evidence: 'Don't replace * with &. & is a restriction, not a join — it would silently change the semantics'.

expectations.text: Does not blame the restriction key (sorting_id is not too loose)
expectations.evidence: 'sorting_id alone is the full PK of SpikeSortingSelection; nothing about the restriction is wrong'.
